TO WHAT EXTENT HAS THE INTRODUCTION OF MINIMUM UNIT PRICING ON

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and consistent communication of the policy topic and tight linkage to the research question throughout the report
Research question clearly stated, focused and repeatedly tied back into each section of the analysis
weaknesses: Methodology, while generally appropriate, lacks justification for source selection and data-validation procedures
Minor repetition of the research-question wording reduces conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates accurate and effective communication of the topic and a sharply focused research question, meriting a high score in Focus and Method.  However, the methodological justification is not fully comprehensive, and occasional digressions and repetitions prevent a top-band score of 6.  A score of 5 reflects the overall coherence and clarity, balanced against those methodological gaps.

strengths: Generally strong integration of official statistics and peer-reviewed sources to support key claims
Correct and coherent application of economic concepts such as externalities, welfareâloss triangles and price elasticity
weaknesses: Use of some loosely connected UK-wide figures and secondary commentary sites without critical appraisal
Minor lapses in conceptual precision (e.g., occasional undefined labels on diagrams)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows a clear, coherent understanding of the topic and effective use of relevant sources, placing the work solidly in the upper half of the Knowledge and Understanding band.  Occasional overreliance on less precise sources and minor conceptual imprecision preclude a perfect score, so a mark of 5 appropriately balances strengths against those weaknesses.

strengths: Research is consistently appropriate and well triangulated (official data, peer-reviewed studies, government evaluations)
Analysis is effective, linking quantitative trends to economic theory and addressing key confounding factors
Arguments are structured and supported by data, with a dedicated limitations section demonstrating critical engagement
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven in places (crime section remains partly descriptive, some confounding effects not fully quantified)
Evaluation would benefit from counterfactual analysis and more systematic measurement of limitation magnitudes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The Critical Thinking criterion is a clear strength of this investigation.  Research quality and relevance consistently exceed expectations, and the student delivers effective analysis with generally well-supported conclusions.  Although a few sections are less critically developed and alternative methods are not fully explored, the overall quality of evaluation and argumentation justifies a highâband score of 10 out of 12.

strengths: Report structure follows all EE conventions (title page, abstract-style introduction, clear headings, contents, bibliography)
Layout is clean, figures are correctly labelled, and in-text citations are consistent
weaknesses: Occasional formatting inconsistencies (bolding, length of figure captions)
Minor slip in the final sentence of the introduction interrupting full coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is a strong aspect of the report, with a clearly appropriate structure and professional layout.  Minor formatting inconsistencies and a small textual error prevent a deduction, but the overall standard warrants the maximum Presentation score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is presented in bold on the title page and re-stated in the introduction and conclusion.  Its wording ("To what extent has the introduction of MUP â¦ been effective â¦?") directs the essay toward a measurable outcome (reduction of negative externalities) and defines clear spatial (Wales) and temporal (post-2020) boundaries.  Throughout the essay the discussion returns to that question when interpreting consumption, hospitalisation and crime data, showing **effective and sustained connection**.  Minor breadth in the phrase ânegative externalitiesâ leaves some room for sharper delineation, hence a score of 5 rather than 6.

The student identifies a precise policy measure (50 p MUP introduced in 2020) and frames the investigation explicitly around its externalities.  The introduction, table of contents and repeated sign-posting keep the reader constantly aware of the purpose and scope, so the topic is **communicated accurately and remains tightly linked to the stated evaluation of welfare effects**.  Minor weaknesses â e.g. some repetition of the RQ wording and occasional digressions into UK-wide figures â prevent a completely seamless focus, but overall clarity and appropriateness clearly exceed the âadequateâ standard.

Official Welsh Government, ONS, Public Health Wales and peer-reviewed Lancet sources are integrated into each section and linked to diagrams and calculations, demonstrating **mostly relevant and appropriate application**.  Nevertheless, some figures (e.g. UK-wide crime statistics) are only loosely connected to the Welsh context, and a few claims rely on secondary commentary sites (Movendi) without critical appraisal, so source use does not reach the consistent precision required for the top band.

The report follows EE conventions: title page, abstract-style introduction, clear headings, numbered pages, contents table and separate bibliography.  Sections flow logically from introduction to methodology, analysis, conclusion and limitations, reflecting **a clearly appropriate structure** with all essential elements present.

Layout is clean, with consistent fonts, correctly labelled figures and in-text citations.  Minor formatting slips (e.g. inconsistent bolding, occasional over-long figure captions) exist but do not impede readability.  Referencing style is mostly consistent, so overall **layout and formatting meet the required conventions effectively**.

The student offers a reasoned overall judgement (âpartially effectiveâ) and a dedicated limitations section that weighs pandemic effects, cross-border shopping and inflation.  This reflects **partially critical evaluation**; however, the discussion stops short of systematically measuring the magnitude of each limitation or exploring alternative policy instruments, so the argumentation, while structured, does not reach the fully critical standard required for band 7-9.

Analysis links trends in consumption, expenditure and hospital admissions to economic theory, includes an elasticity computation, and discusses confounding factors such as COVID-19 and inflation.  Conclusions are mostly supported by data and diagrams, demonstrating **clear and largely coherent analysis**.  Nonetheless, some arguments remain descriptive (e.g. crime section) and counter-arguments are not fully quantified, capping the depth at the top of the 4-6 band.

The essay shows solid knowledge of externalities, price floors and PED, correctly applying concepts such as welfare loss triangles and elasticity.  Explanations are coherent and usually supported by data.  **Occasional simplifications** (e.g. equating MSC with MPC post-policy without qualification) and some descriptive passages about legislation indicate understanding that is clear but not consistently sophisticated.

The majority of the researchâofficial statistics, peer-reviewed studies and government evaluationsâis directly relevant to the RQ and triangulated across time periods.  The student deliberately omits anecdotal media sources and acknowledges data lags, showing **clear evidence of appropriateness and relevance in most cases**.  A handful of less-scholarly sources (blog posts) and limited primary data collection prevent the highest level but the criterion comfortably exceeds the 4-6 band.

The methodology section explains search strategies (Google Scholar, official statistics), inclusion criteria (post-2018, Wales-specific) and basic data handling (Excel, graphical presentation).  Sources cited are generally reputable and relevant; however, **there is limited justification of why particular data series were preferred, no explanation of reliability checks, and only one simple elasticity calculation** is proposed as an analytical tool.  The range is therefore âgenerally appropriateâ rather than fully comprehensive or demonstrably âeffective and informedâ.

Key economic termsânegative externality, social optimum, PED, demerit goodâare used correctly and appear in diagrams and prose.  Notation is generally accurate.  **Minor lapses** (e.g. occasional use of MSC/MPB labels without defining MSB) show room for greater consistency, placing the work securely in the âmostly accurateâ rather than âfully consistentâ level.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated with defined spatial and temporal boundaries, effectively guiding the investigation and maintaining focus throughout the essay.
The word count of 3824 is clearly provided at the start of the report, fully meeting the IB formatting requirement.
Page numbers have not been added to the footer; including consecutive page numbers would improve navigability and meet IB conventions.
The table of contents comprehensively lists all sections with accurate page references, enhancing the reportâs overall structure and readability.
The methodology outlines search keywords and databases but omits justification for selecting these criteria and lacks description of data validation procedures.
The data collection process is clearly described; however, including rationale for choosing specific datasets and detailing checks on data reliability would enhance methodological rigor.
The overview of the MUP policyâs legislative framework is clearly presented, demonstrating effective application of source material to explain context.
The calculation formula is accurate, but clarify unit conversions consistently (e.g., litres vs mL) to avoid ambiguity in the calculation example.
The calculation table is clear, but ensure numerical formatting is consistent (e.g., two decimal places for volumes) to maintain precision.
Strong use of official statistics to contextualize alcohol consumption and mortality rates; consider explicitly linking each figure to a specific negative externality in your discussion.
Figure 1 is clearly labelled, but quantifying the percentage increase in death rates after MUP introduction would strengthen your critical analysis of the trend.
The final sentence of the introduction is incomplete; revise to fully articulate the essayâs aims and ensure a coherent transition into the main analysis.
Repetition of the research question reinforces focus, but reducing duplication between the introduction and body will improve conciseness.
Figure 6 presents self-reported behavior effectively, but addressing survey bias and response rates would strengthen your evaluation of the dataâs reliability.
The contrast between self-reported and actual consumption trends is noted; further exploring reasons for this discrepancy would deepen critical evaluation.
While the regional comparison is informative, adding a temporal component (pre- and post-MUP) for Wales in this chart would directly address the research question.
The market failure diagram is well-constructed; adding a legend or annotations to define MPC and clarify why MSC overlaps with MPC would improve conceptual accuracy.
The section on demerit goods accurately links theory to the MUP policy; referencing foundational work on negative externalities (e.g., Pigou) could deepen the academic discussion.
Figure 5 effectively displays consumption trends, but discussing potential margins of error or confidence intervals would deepen the critical evaluation of the data.
The introductory statement in the analysis section clearly ties consumption trends back to the MUP policy, maintaining strong alignment with the research question.
The PED calculation is correct, but discussing differences in elasticity between heavy and light drinkers would provide more nuanced insight into consumer responsiveness.
Acknowledging COVID-19âs confounding effect is important; including a control series or counterfactual analysis would clarify its specific impact separate from MUP.
Attributing higher per-capita purchases in Wales to cultural preferences is plausible, but citing supporting studies or controlling for socioeconomic factors would substantiate the claim.
The spending trend figure is valuable, yet clarify whether the axis represents fiscal or calendar years and ensure consistent axis scaling for accurate comparisons.
The stockpiling hypothesis is insightful; adjusting nominal expenditure for inflation would help isolate the real effect of MUP on spending patterns.
